text: ‘I heard another, too, saying from the altar’; in the Greek, ‘I heard, too, the altar saying’.
‘His false prophet’; that is, apparently, the second Beast (13.11).
This verse is a parenthesis, which insists upon the suddenness of God’s visitations; cf. Mt. 24.18; II Cor. 5.3.
The Latin here has ‘he will gather them’; in the Greek, ‘they gathered them’.
‘The great city’ may be Jerusalem (11.8); but some commentators understand a double reference in this verse to Babylon, that is, Rome.
